edge_hypothesis: The only profitable outcome in 60+ cycles (PF 1.033, 389 trades) came from EXIT_CHANGE, not signal improvement. This means the pipeline's entire signal (ema_rsi_atr) may have marginal edge that is destroyed by poor exit parameters. EMA 9/21 crossover is the simplest, cleanest trend signal with the least overfitting risk. By pairing it with the exit optimization insight — wider stops (2.0-2.5x ATR) and asymmetric TP (3.0x ATR) — we test whether a clean signal with optimized exits can outperform the more complex ema_rsi_atr. The hypothesis: the edge is in exit management, not entry complexity. EMA crossover fires fewer, higher-conviction signals than ema_rsi_atr's continuous RSI filtering.
regime_assumption: Works in trending and transitional regimes where directional moves develop. ADX > 20 gate filters ranging chop. The PF 1.033 outcome showed balanced performance across trending (1.054) and transitional (1.048) regimes — exits matter more than regime selection when the signal is clean.

variants.name: ema_cross_exit_optimized
variants.description: EMA 9/21 crossover with exit parameters derived from the PF 1.033 exit_change insight. Wider stops (2.0x ATR) and asymmetric TP (3.0x ATR) replicate the winning exit profile. ETH/1h for direct comparison to the profitable outcome.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
Require ADX_14 > 20 to confirm trending regime.
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
Require ADX_14 > 20 to confirm trending regime.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Exit optimization: derived from PF 1.033 exit_change insight on family 6167b17afa4f
exit_rules: Exit on opposite EMA 9/21 crossover (signal reversal).
Exit on ADX_14 dropping below 15 (regime invalidation).
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (20 bars).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Late crossover after extended trend run
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Whipsaw during low-ADX consolidation
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 from entry (wider stop from exit_change insight).
Take profit at 3.0x ATR_14 from entry (1.5:1 R:R — asymmetric payoff).
Exit optimization note: PF 1.033 outcome used wider stops that avoided premature stop-outs during regime transitions.

components.notes: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover as clean entry signal — simplest trend signal
EMA_9 vs EMA_21 relative position confirms direction
ATR_14 for exit-optimized stop/TP sizing, ADX_14 > 20 trending gate

variants.name: ema_cross_wide_exit_eth4h
variants.description: EMA crossover on ETH/4h with even wider exits (2.5x stop, 4.0x TP) for 1.6:1 R:R. 4h timeframe reduces noise and produces fewer, higher-quality crossover signals. Tests whether longer timeframe + wider exits amplifies the exit_change edge.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
Require ADX_14 > 20 to confirm trending regime.
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
Require ADX_14 > 20 to confirm trending regime.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on opposite EMA 9/21 crossover (signal reversal).
Exit on ADX_14 dropping below 15 (regime invalidation).
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (15 bars on 4h = 60 hours).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 4h bar close misses intra-bar reversal
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Low trade count produces unreliable statistics
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 2.5x ATR_14 from entry (wide stop for 4h timeframe).
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry (1.6:1 R:R).
4h timeframe note: fewer signals, wider ATR means larger nominal stop — acceptable on ETH.

components.notes: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on 4h — cleaner signal, less noise
ATR_14 for wide exit sizing, ADX_14 > 20 trending gate

variants.name: ema_cross_no_regime_gate
variants.description: Control variant: EMA crossover with exit optimization but NO ADX regime gate. Tests whether the exit parameters alone (without regime filtering) produce positive expectancy. If this variant matches the gated variants, it proves exits matter more than regime selection.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
No regime gate — trade all market conditions.
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
No regime gate — trade all market conditions.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Control variant: no ADX regime gate — tests exit params in isolation
exit_rules: Exit on opposite EMA 9/21 crossover (signal reversal).
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (20 bars).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Excessive whipsaw in ranging market without regime gate
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Drawdown accumulation during extended chop
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 from entry (exit-optimized).
Take profit at 3.0x ATR_14 from entry (1.5:1 R:R).
Control variant: no regime gate to isolate exit parameter effect.

components.notes: ATR_14 for exit-optimized stop/TP sizing only
